content.problemStatement:
### **Aptitude Practice Question**

If the difference between compound interest and simple interest on some principle amount is at the rate of interest of 20% per annum for 3 years in $48, then what is the principle amount?

---

### **Answer & Explanation**

Here, T = 3 years, R = 20%, P = ?
Also, C.I - S.I = 48
P(1 + (R / 100))T - P - (P × R × T) / 100 = 48 
⇒ P(1 + (20/100))3 - P - (P × 20 × 3) / 100 = 48
⇒ P(6/5)3 - P - P (3/5) = 48 
⇒ P ((216/125)3 - 1 - (3/5)) = 48
⇒ P (0.128) = 48
⇒ P = (48/0.128)
⇒ P = 375

Therefore, the principle amount is $375.
